page: The Vultr vbm-6c-32gb is a dedicated bare-metal server belonging to the Bare Metal SSD family. It is powered by an Intel Xeon 2286G processor utilizing the x86_64 architecture. This CPU features 6 physical cores and 12 vCPUs with hyper-threading enabled, operating at a base clock speed of 4.0 GHz. The processor includes a 12 MB L3 cache and supports AVX, AES, and VMX virtualization flags. The server is equipped with 32.0 GB of system memory, providing 2.67 GB of RAM per core. Storage is provided via a bundled 1920 GB local SSD, and the system includes one complimentary public IPv4 address. No GPU is present on this configuration.

In benchmark evaluations, the server demonstrates strong single-threaded performance, highlighted by a top-tier gzip compression speed of 35.51 GB/sec and a strong bzip3 single-threaded decompression rate of 13.91 GB/sec. General CPU capabilities are solid, with a passmark CPU score of 15132.19 and a stress-ng multi-core performance score of 15194.54, both falling within the average tier. Memory performance presents a clear tradeoff: while the PassMark memory score is top-tier at 3453.69 and small-block cached read bandwidth is average at 851.86 GB/sec, large-block uncached read and write bandwidths are weak, measuring 32.51 GB/sec and 14.71 GB/sec respectively.

This bare-metal configuration offers high resource density and dedicated compute performance, making it a cost-efficient option for workloads requiring raw CPU power without virtualization overhead. The combination of a high 4.0 GHz clock speed and a 1920 GB local SSD makes the server well-suited for database operations, static web serving, and moderate file-processing tasks. However, due to weak large-block memory bandwidth and low performance on larger models, such as the 7B LLM prompt processing at 25.26 tokens/sec, it is less suitable for memory-intensive data science or large-scale LLM inference.
description: The Vultr vbm-6c-32gb is a bare-metal server powered by an Intel Xeon 2286G x86_64 processor with 6 physical cores, 12 vCPUs, and a high clock speed of 4.0 GHz. It features 32.0 GB of RAM and a bundled 1920 GB local SSD, with no GPU. Performance benchmarks show top-tier single-threaded gzip compression (35.51 GB/sec) and strong single-threaded bzip3 decompression, alongside average multi-core CPU and database performance. However, a key tradeoff is its weak large-block uncached memory read and write bandwidth. This server is a cost-efficient, dedicated hardware option for workloads that benefit from high clock speeds and local storage, such as relational databases, static web serving, and localized application hosting, while being less optimal for memory-intensive data science or large LLM inference.
og_description: Vultr vbm-6c-32gb is a bare-metal server featuring an Intel Xeon 2286G CPU with 12 vCPUs, 32 GB RAM, and a 1920 GB SSD. It offers strong single-threaded performance for databases and web serving.
meta_description: Vultr vbm-6c-32gb is a bare-metal server with an Intel Xeon CPU, 12 vCPUs, 32 GB RAM, and a 1920 GB SSD, optimized for databases and web serving.
tagline: A dedicated bare-metal server featuring high-frequency processing, substantial local SSD storage, and strong single-threaded performance for localized workloads.
bullet_points: Intel Xeon 2286G processor with 6 physical cores, 12 vCPUs, and 4.0 GHz clock speed
Bundled 1920 GB local SSD storage and 32.0 GB of system memory
Top-tier single-threaded gzip compression and strong bzip3 decompression performance
Tradeoff of weak large-block uncached memory read and write bandwidth
Best suited for relational databases, static web serving, and dedicated application hosting
